<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Get the singleton observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // Trigger notification by changing the target
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Return notification status and call count
    return std::make_pair(observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount());
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// Data model definitions
class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
        // Remove this observer from the graph to prevent future notifications
        auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
        graph.removeObserver(this, nullptr); // Assuming removeObserver takes (observer, target)
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

// Function implementation
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_unique<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_unique<TestObserverReset>(targetName);

    // Get the singleton ObserverGraph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Establish observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(observer.get(), target.get());

    // Notify the target and verify observer was notified
    target->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Record call count before reset
    int initialCallCount = observer->getCallCount();

    // Reset the observer node
    observer->reset();

    // Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);

    // Verify observer was not notified after reset
    if (observer->getCallCount() != initialCallCount) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace reaction {
    class ObserverNode {
    public:
        virtual ~ObserverNode() = default;
        virtual void valueChanged(bool changed = true) {
            // Base implementation for propagation
        }
        void addObserver(std::shared_ptr<ObserverNode> observer) {
            // Implementation to add observer
        }
        void close() {
            // Implementation to close node and remove from graph
        }
    };

    class ObserverGraph {
    public:
        static ObserverGraph& getInstance() {
            static ObserverGraph instance;
            return instance;
        }
    };
}

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Create source and target nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName + "_source");
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName + "_target");

    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: source is observed by target
    source->addObserver(target);

    // Notify source (should notify target)
    source->valueChanged(true);

    // Verify target was notified
    if (!target->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Reset target state by creating a new target node
    target = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName + "_target");

    // Close source node
    source->close();

    // Notify source again
    source->valueChanged(true);

    // Verify target was not notified after closure
    if (target->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_update_chain(int a, int b, int newA) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    
    // Create first calculation dependent on both variables
    auto calc1 = reaction::calc([&]() { 
        return var_a.get() + var_b.get(); 
    });
    
    // Create second calculation dependent on first calculation
    auto calc2 = reaction::calc([&]() { 
        return calc1.get() * 2; 
    });
    
    // Update the first variable
    var_a.set(newA);
    
    // Return the final result of the chain
    return calc2.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age) 
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}
    
    const reaction::Var<std::string>& getName() const { return m_name; }
    const reaction::Var<int>& getAge() const { return m_age; }
    
    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.set(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.set(age); }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    
    auto info = reaction::calc([&person]() {
        return person.getName().get() + " - " + std::to_string(person.getAge().get());
    });
    
    person.setName(newName);
    person.setAge(newAge);
    
    return info.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_batch_execution` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variables
2. Create variable to track recomputation count
3. Create calculation
4. Perform non-batch updates
5. Record recompute count after non-batch updates
6. Create batch context
7. Execute batch
8. Record recompute count after batch updates
9. Return final sum and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    
    // Track recomputation count
    int recompute_count = 0;
    auto sum_calc = reaction::calc([a, b, &recompute_count]() {
        int result = a.get() + b.get();
        return result;
    });
    
    // Set up observer for recomputation tracking
    sum_calc.observe([&recompute_count](int old_val, int new_val) {
        recompute_count++;
    });
    
    // Perform non-batch updates
    a.set(nonBatchA);
    b.set(nonBatchB);
    int non_batch_count = recompute_count;
    
    // Perform batch updates
    reaction::Batch batch;
    batch.execute([&]() {
        a.set(batchA);
        b.set(batchB);
    });
    
    int batch_count = recompute_count;
    
    return {sum_calc.get(), non_batch_count, batch_count};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_filter_trigger` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variable
2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
3. Create filter-triggered calculation
4. Apply filter
5. Update sensor with first value
6. Record recompute count after first update
7. Update sensor with second value
8. Record recompute count after second update
9. Get final status
10. Return final status and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    
    // Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto recomputeCount = reaction::var(0);
    
    // Create calculation that depends on reactiveVar and updates recomputeCount
    auto calculation = reaction::calc(reactiveVar,
        [deltaA, deltaB](int val) { return val + deltaA + deltaB; },
        [recomputeCount](int) { recomputeCount.set(recomputeCount.get() + 1); }
    );
    
    // Record recompute count before batch execution
    int recomputeCountBefore = recomputeCount.get();
    
    // Execute batch updates
    reaction::batch([&]() {
        reactiveVar.set(baseValue + deltaA);
        reactiveVar.set(reactiveVar.get() + deltaB);
    });
    
    // Record recompute count after batch execution
    int recomputeCountAfter = recomputeCount.get();
    
    // Get the final value of the calculation
    int finalValue = calculation.get();
    
    return BatchExecuteResult{
        .recomputeCountBeforeBatch = recomputeCountBefore,
        .recomputeCountAfterBatch = recomputeCountAfter,
        .finalValue = finalValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <functional>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchLifecycleResult {
    bool closedInitially;
    bool closedAfterClose;
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute;
};

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    BatchLifecycleResult result;
    
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto var = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // 3. Create calculation that depends on 'var'
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&recomputeCount](int value) {
        recomputeCount++;
        return value * 2; // Example computation
    }, var);
    
    // 4. Create Batch object
    reaction::Batch batch;
    
    // 5. Check if batch is closed initially
    result.closedInitially = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 6. Add operation to update variable and execute batch
    batch.add([&var, updatedValue]() {
        var.set(updatedValue);
    });
    batch.execute();
    
    // 7. Record recompute count after execute
    result.recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCount;
    
    // 8. Close batch
    batch.close();
    
    // 9. Check if batch is closed after close
    result.closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <unordered_set>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        reaction::ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    graph.registerNode(nodeA);
    graph.registerNode(nodeB);
    graph.registerNode(nodeC);

    nodeB->addDirectObserver(nodeA);
    nodeC->addDirectObserver(nodeB);

    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    graph.collectObservers(nodeB, observers);

    nodeB->valueChanged(true);
    nodeC->valueChanged(true);

    auto statsBefore = graph.getStats();
    graph.triggerCleanup();
    auto statsAfter = graph.getStats();

    double hitRatio = statsAfter.totalEntries > 0 ? 
        static_cast<double>(statsAfter.hits) / statsAfter.totalEntries : 0.0;

    nodeA->close();
    nodeB->close();
    nodeC->close();

    return CacheStatsSnapshot{statsBefore.totalEntries, statsAfter.totalEntries, hitRatio};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {
    auto var = reaction::var(initialValue);
    auto counter = reaction::var(0);
    auto calc = reaction::calc([var, counter]() {
        counter.set(counter.get() + 1);
        return var.get() * 2;
    }, var);

    int recomputeCountAfterInit = counter.get();

    var.set(initialValue);

    // Force evaluation to trigger the calculation and avoid unused variable warnings
    int dummy = calc.get();

    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = counter.get();

    return AlwaysTriggerResult{
        recomputeCountAfterInit,
        recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

class IdTrackedFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_value;

public:
    explicit IdTrackedFields(int value) : m_value(value) {}
    
    reaction::Var<int>& valueField() { return m_value; }
    
    uint64_t getId() const { 
        // Assuming FieldBase provides a unique ID mechanism
        return reinterpret_cast<uint64_t>(const_cast<IdTrackedFields*>(this));
    }
};

struct FieldIdComparison {
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead;
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead;
    uint64_t secondId;
};

FieldIdComparison test_field_unique_id(int initialFirst, int initialSecond) {
    auto first = IdTrackedFields(initialFirst);
    auto second = IdTrackedFields(initialSecond);
    
    // Update reactive fields to avoid unused variable warnings
    first.valueField().set(initialFirst + 1);
    second.valueField().set(initialSecond + 1);
    
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead = first.getId();
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead = first.getId();
    uint64_t secondId = second.getId();
    
    return FieldIdComparison{firstIdFirstRead, firstIdSecondRead, secondId};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConditionalMutexResult {
    bool tryLockWithoutSafety;
    bool tryLockWithSafety;
};

ConditionalMutexResult test_conditional_mutex_behavior() {
    auto &tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();

    reaction::ConditionalMutex mutex;

    bool tryLockWithoutSafety = false;
    {
        reaction::ConditionalLockGuard guard(mutex);
        tryLockWithoutSafety = mutex.tryLock(false);
    }

    tm.enableThreadSafety(true);

    bool tryLockWithSafety = false;
    {
        reaction::ConditionalLockGuard guard(mutex);
        tryLockWithSafety = mutex.tryLock(true);
    }

    return {tryLockWithoutSafety, tryLockWithSafety};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>

std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(
    const std::vector<reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode> &codes) {
    std::vector<std::string> descriptions;
    descriptions.reserve(codes.size());

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ExceptionInfo {
    std::string formattedMessage;
    std::string originalMessage;
    std::string file;
    int line;
    std::string function;
    std::string codeName;
};

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    
    reaction::ReactionException ex(message, code, file, line, function);
    ExceptionInfo info;
    
    info.formattedMessage = ex.getFormattedMessage();
    info.originalMessage = ex.getOriginalMessage();
    info.file = ex.getFileName();
    info.line = ex.getLineNumber();
    info.function = ex.getFunctionName();
    info.codeName = ex.getErrorCodeName();
    
    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

struct OrderingSnapshot {

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables with assigned names
    auto var1 = React::var(firstName);
    auto var2 = React::var(secondName);
    auto var3 = React::var(thirdName);
    
    // Insert into a set (automatically ordered using React::operator<)
    std::set<React> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect ordered names from the set
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }
    
    // Check if all collected names are unique
    std::set<std::string> uniqueNames(orderedNames.begin(), orderedNames.end());
    bool allUnique = (uniqueNames.size() == orderedNames.size());
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        if (changed) {
            m_notifications++;
        }
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }
    
    void clear() {
        m_notifications = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    auto owner = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto field = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("field");

    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();

    observerGraph.registerNode(owner);
    observerGraph.registerNode(field);

    fieldGraph.registerObject(objectId, field);

    observerGraph.addDependency(owner, field);

    field->valueChanged(true);
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete = (owner->notifications() > 0);

    owner->clear();
    observerGraph.clearDependencies(owner);
    fieldGraph.removeObject(objectId);

    observerGraph.addDependency(owner, field);

    field->valueChanged(true);
    bool notifiedAfterDelete = (owner->notifications() > 0);

    owner->close();
    field->close();

    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBeforeDelete, notifiedAfterDelete};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    auto source1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(observer);
    graph.registerNode(source1);
    graph.registerNode(source2);
    graph.registerNode(source3);

    graph.attach(source1, observer);

    source1->notify(true);
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial = observer->notifications();

    // Rewire: detach from source1 and attach to source2 and source3
    graph.detach(source1, observer);
    graph.attach(source2, observer);
    graph.attach(source3, observer);

    source1->notify(true);
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = observer->notifications();

    source2->notify(true);
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = observer->notifications();

    source3->notify(true);
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = observer->notifications();

    source1->close();
    source2->close();
    source3->close();
    observer->close();

    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        firstNotificationsAfterInitial,
        firstNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        secondNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LogicalExpressionSnapshot {
    bool initialAnd;
    bool initialOr;
    bool updatedAnd;
    bool updatedOr;
};

inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {
    // Create reactive boolean variables
    auto a_var = reaction::var(a);
    auto b_var = reaction::var(b);
    auto c_var = reaction::var(c);
    
    // Build logical AND and OR expressions using library functions
    auto and_expr = reaction::logical_and(a_var, b_var);
    auto or_expr = reaction::logical_or(a_var, c_var);
    
    // Get initial results
    bool initialAnd = and_expr.get();
    bool initialOr = or_expr.get();
    
    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    a_var.set(!a);
    c_var.set(!c);
    
    // Get updated results
    bool updatedAnd = and_expr.get();
    bool updatedOr = or_expr.get();
    
    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,
        .initialOr = initialOr,
        .updatedAnd = updatedAnd,
        .updatedOr = updatedOr
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    class IntCache : public CacheBase<int, int> {
    public:
        using CacheBase<int, int>::insert;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::lookup;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::invalidateAll;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::triggerCleanup;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::getStats;
    };

    IntCache cache;
    
    // 4. Get initial cache statistics
    auto initialStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesBefore = initialStats.entries;
    
    // 5. Perform initial lookup (key not in cache)
    auto firstResult = cache.lookup(42);
    bool firstWasMiss = !firstResult.isHit();
    
    // 6. Insert value into cache
    cache.insert(42, 100);
    
    // 7. Perform second lookup (key now in cache)
    auto secondResult = cache.lookup(42);
    bool secondWasHit = secondResult.isHit();
    
    // 8. Get cache statistics after insertion
    auto postInsertStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert = postInsertStats.entries;
    std::size_t hits = postInsertStats.hitCount;
    std::size_t misses = postInsertStats.missCount;
    double hitRatio = (hits + misses) > 0 ? static_cast<double>(hits) / (hits + misses) : 0.0;
    
    // 9. Invalidate all cache entries
    cache.invalidateAll();
    
    // 10. Trigger cleanup
    cache.triggerCleanup();
    
    // 11. Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto postInvalidationStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = postInvalidationStats.entries;
    
    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        entriesBefore,
        entriesAfterInsert,
        entriesAfterInvalidate,
        hits,
        misses,
        hitRatio,
        firstWasMiss,
        secondWasHit
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

So steps 6-7: create worker thread, register it, then wait for completion.

Finally, check thread safety after worker registration and store in `safetyAfterWorkerForce`.

Putting it all together:

2. Reset: tm.reset();
3. Check safety before: bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
4. Register main thread: tm.registerCurrentThread();
5. Check safety after main: bool safetyAfterMain = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
6. Create worker thread: std::thread worker([&tm]() { tm.registerCurrentThread(); });
7. Wait for worker: worker.join();
8. Check safety after worker: bool safetyAfterWorker = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
9. Return snapshot with these three booleans.

Wait, but the worker thread function captures the thread manager instance by reference. But since it's a singleton, maybe we don't need to pass it. Alternatively, the worker thread can get its own instance via `ThreadManager::getInstance()`, which would be the same object. So perhaps the worker function can be written as:

    tm.registerCurrentThread();
};

That way, no need to capture the instance from the main thread. That's better because it avoids potential issues with references.

So the code would look like:

    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.registerCurrentThread();
});

Then, after creating the thread, join it.

So putting all together:

```cpp
#include <thread>
#include <vector> // for std::thread (if needed)

namespace test_reaction {

struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    
    bool safetyBeforeForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    tm.registerCurrentThread(); // main thread
    
    bool safetyAfterMainForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    std::thread worker([]() {
        auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
        tm.registerCurrentThread();
    });
    
    worker.join();
    
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ForceRegisterSnapshot{
        safetyBeforeForce,
        safetyAfterMainForce,
        safetyAfterWorkerForce
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```

Assuming that `ThreadManager` has `reset()`, `registerCurrentThread()`, and `isThreadSafetyEnabled()`, the code should work.

Another thing to consider: when the worker thread registers, does that trigger the thread safety mechanism? The idea is that when multiple threads register, the thread manager enables thread safety. So initially, after main thread registration, safety is still false (since only one thread). After worker thread registers, safety becomes true.

So the expected values would be:

safetyAfterMainForce: false (since only main thread)

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    
    bool safetyBeforeForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    tm.registerCurrentThread(); // Main thread registration
    
    bool safetyAfterMainForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    std::thread worker([]() {
        auto& tm_worker = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
        tm_worker.registerCurrentThread(); // Worker thread registration
    });
    
    worker.join();
    
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ForceRegisterSnapshot{
        safetyBeforeForce,
        safetyAfterMainForce,
        safetyAfterWorkerForce
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_raw_expression_builders` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variables
2. Build sum expression
3. Build negated expression
4. Build chained expression
5. Get initial expression values
6. Update variables to trigger recomputation
7. Get updated expression values
8. Return raw expression snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
